title: Build the sandboxed Windows Electron shell
summary: Wrap the Web client in Electron with context isolation, sandboxing, minimal preload ports and a smoke build; do not expose arbitrary Node APIs.
acceptance: nodeIntegration is disabled and contextIsolation/sandbox are enabled
Preload exposes only typed placeholder platform ports
Development and packaged smoke builds launch on Windows CI or an approved equivalent
Security checklist has no unresolved high finding

history.details: Built sandboxed Windows Electron shell with typed preload ports, custom unpacked packaging, Windows smoke coverage, and zero high security findings.

changes_requested: High: packaged Electron still trusts WM_DESKTOP_DEV_SERVER_URL for the main renderer URL, allowing packaged launches to load arbitrary remote/dev content with the preload bridge and leaving packaged smoke unable to prove file renderer loading. Medium: package-dir copies the whole dist/main directory without cleaning it, so stale compiled files such as desktop-shell.e2e.test.js leaked into win-unpacked during review build.

Fixed packaged renderer trust boundary so packaged Electron always loads file content, cleaned/whitelisted main bundle outputs, and added smoke coverage proving dev URL is ignored and stale compiled tests stay out of win-unpacked.

review.summary: High: packaged Electron still trusts WM_DESKTOP_DEV_SERVER_URL for the main renderer URL, allowing packaged launches to load arbitrary remote/dev content with the preload bridge and leaving packaged smoke unable to prove file renderer loading. Medium: package-dir copies the whole dist/main directory without cleaning it, so stale compiled files such as desktop-shell.e2e.test.js leaked into win-unpacked during review build.
